title: Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese
description: Palak Paneer is a vibrant North Indian dish featuring tender spinach puree and succulent cubes of Indian cottage cheese (paneer). It's a comforting, nutrient-rich vegetarian curry with earthy, savory flavors balanced by aromatic spices, making it a beloved staple across India.

ingredients.notes: or as needed for gravy consistency

instructions.title: Prepare the Spinach
instructions.text: Wash the spinach thoroughly. Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the spinach and blanch for 1-2 minutes until wilted and bright green. Immediately drain and transfer the spinach to a bowl of ice-cold water to stop the cooking process and retain its vibrant color. Once cooled, squeeze out excess water and blend the spinach into a smooth puree.

instructions.title: Prepare the Paneer
instructions.text: Cut the paneer into 1-inch cubes. You can use it as is, or for a slightly firmer texture and golden color, lightly pan-fry the paneer cubes in a little ghee or oil until they are lightly golden on all sides. Set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat ghee or oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or kadai over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Then add the finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, cooking for another 1-2 minutes until the raw aroma disappears.

instructions.title: Build the Masala Base
instructions.text: Add the tomato puree to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes until it thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides. Now, add turmeric powder, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix well and cook the spices for 2-3 minutes on low heat, stirring continuously, until the masala is fragrant and the oil glistens.

instructions.title: Combine Spinach and Masala
instructions.text: Pour the spinach puree into the pan with the cooked masala. Mix everything thoroughly. Add about 1/4 cup of water to adjust the consistency; the gravy should be thick but pourable. Bring it to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Be careful as spinach can splatter while simmering.

instructions.title: Add Paneer and Finish
instructions.text: Gently add the paneer cubes to the simmering spinach gravy. Add the garam masala and crushed Kasuri Methi. Stir gently to combine. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes, just enough for the paneer to absorb the flavors without becoming rubbery. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Garnish with a swirl of fresh cream if desired before serving.

tips: For the brightest green color, always use an ice bath immediately after blanching the spinach. This stops the cooking process and locks in the chlorophyll.
Do not overcook the paneer once it's added to the gravy; a gentle simmer for a few minutes is sufficient to absorb flavors without making it tough or rubbery.
Cook the onion-tomato masala until the oil separates from the mixture – this indicates that the spices are well cooked and the base is flavorful, avoiding any raw taste.
article:
## History of Palak Paneer Spinach Cottage Cheese

Palak Paneer, a quintessential North Indian vegetarian dish, traces its origins to the fertile plains of Punjab and Uttar Pradesh, where fresh spinach and dairy products like paneer (Indian cottage cheese) have been staples for centuries. While the exact genesis is debated, the combination of a vibrant green leafy vegetable with a protein-rich cheese makes it a nutritious and satisfying meal. It evolved from simpler preparations of greens (saag) and local cheeses, eventually formalizing into the beloved dish we know today. Over time, it gained immense popularity across India and among the Indian diaspora, becoming a standard offering in Indian restaurants worldwide and a cherished home-cooked meal, symbolizing comfort and traditional Indian flavors. Its preparation reflects the rich culinary heritage of the region, emphasizing fresh ingredients and aromatic spices.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe achieves its authentic, rich flavor and vibrant color through a meticulous layering of traditional Indian techniques. Blanching the spinach briefly in hot water followed by an ice bath is crucial; it locks in the bright green hue and prevents oxidation, ensuring the final dish looks as appealing as it tastes. The slow cooking of the onion-tomato masala base allows the flavors of ginger, garlic, and dry spices to meld deeply, creating a robust foundation. The paneer, whether lightly pan-fried or added raw, absorbs these aromatic flavors beautifully while maintaining its soft texture. The addition of Kasuri Methi and garam masala at the final stage infuses the dish with an irresistible, earthy fragrance, elevating it from a simple spinach curry to a gourmet experience.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

For a truly outstanding Palak Paneer, always start with the freshest spinach available; its quality directly impacts the dish's flavor and color. When blanching, do not overcook the spinach – just a minute or two is enough to tenderize it without losing its vibrant green. Immediately plunge it into ice-cold water to halt the cooking process and preserve the color. For the masala, patience is key; cook the onion-tomato base until the oil separates, indicating that the spices are fully cooked and their raw aroma has vanished. If you prefer a smoother texture, pass the spinach puree through a fine-mesh sieve or blend it until absolutely smooth. Finally, resist the urge to overcook the paneer once added to the gravy, as it can become rubbery; a gentle simmer for just a few minutes is sufficient for it to absorb the flavors.

## Serving Suggestions

Palak Paneer is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice preparations. For a classic North Indian meal, serve it hot with freshly made Tandoori Roti, soft Naan, or crispy Laccha Paratha. The rich, earthy flavors of the Palak Paneer complement the textures of these breads perfectly. Alternatively, it can be enjoyed with steamed Basmati rice, Jeera Rice (cumin rice), or even a simple Pulao for a more complete and satisfying meal. For a balanced thali, accompany it with a side of cooling Raita (yogurt dip), a simple dal, and a fresh salad. A squeeze of fresh lime juice just before serving can brighten the flavors further, adding a delightful tang.

1 tsp Red Chilli Powder (adjust to taste)
1/4 cup Water (or as needed for gravy consistency)
